etymology.description: Recorded as an ancient form of 龍 -> standardized as 竜 in Japanese -> used alongside simplified form 龙 in contemporary usage
etymology.interpretation: The character 竜 was originally recorded as an ancient form of 龍 (lóng, "dragon") in early dictionaries. It was formally attested in the Sui dynasty epitaph and has been used as a standard form in Japanese, reflecting earlier shapes and developments seen in the Chinese character 龍, symbolized by a composite creature combining different animal features. The evolution displays a continuity in representing the mythical dragon across cultures, maintaining the imagery of a sacred animal throughout history.

examples.chinese: 竜树下(竜樹下)，我们常常聚集(我們常常聚集)。
examples.pinyin: Zhú shù xià, wǒmen chángcháng jùjí.
examples.english: Under the pipal tree, we often gather.

examples.chinese: 庭院中有一棵古老的竜(庭院中有一棵古老的竜)，它见证了很多历史(它見證了很多歷史)。
examples.pinyin: Tíngyuàn zhōng yǒu yī kē gǔlǎo de zhú, tā jiànzhèngle hěnduō lìshǐ.
examples.english: In the courtyard, there is an ancient pipal tree that has witnessed much history.

examples.chinese: 孩子们喜欢在竜的阴影下玩耍(孩子們喜歡在竜的陰影下玩耍)。
examples.pinyin: Háizimen xǐhuān zài zhú de yīnyǐng xià wánshuǎ.
examples.english: The children like to play under the shade of the pipal tree.
